config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa pagar yang jelas, itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum bagi tiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dijaga, bukan dikorbankan begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how quickly that idea can be used to justify abuse, censorship, or discrimination. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case: public safety, public health, and basic rules of coexistence do sometimes require limits, like quarantine, taxes, or laws against harm. The key difference is that those limits should be narrow, justified, and accountable, not a blank check for the majority or the government to override people whenever it is convenient. So for me, the default should be protecting the individual, with society given priority only in clearly defined exceptions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, entah itu terhadap kelompok kecil, kebebasan berpendapat, atau akses yang adil terhadap hukum. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau kesehatan umum yang perlu pembatasan, tetapi itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan jadi alasan kosong untuk menekan orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir ketika hak individu dilindungi dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dibangun tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in the United States we have too many examples of “society first” turning into censorship, forced conformity, or discrimination against unpopular minorities. That said, I also do not believe individual rights should be treated as untouchable in every circumstance, since things like public health, emergency response, and preventing direct harm sometimes justify limited restrictions. What matters is whether the restriction is narrow, evidence-based, temporary, and subject to real oversight, not whether it sounds noble on paper. So my view is that individual rights should be the default priority, and society should override them only in exceptional cases with strong justification.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup-nutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa dan membungkam yang lemah. Hak individu memang bukan mutlak tanpa batas, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Kalau kepentingan sosial selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak orang per orang, yang paling sering dikorbankan justru minoritas dan warga yang tidak punya pengaruh. Bagi saya, kepentingan bersama yang sehat justru lahir dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, bukan dari pengorbanan hak itu sejak awal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general principle, because in the United States "society first" has too often been used to excuse censorship, coercion, and mistreatment of unpopular groups. At the same time, I do not treat individual rights as limitless; public health, emergency response, and preventing direct harm can justify narrow restrictions when they are evidence-based and accountable. What I cannot accept is the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because that gives the majority too much power to decide whose rights are expendable. To me, the safer rule is that individual rights come first by default, and any exception has to be tightly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
